task_completion.description: Verify the rocket's fins have been redesigned to a swept and tapered planform.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In OpenRocket, the fin configuration dialog shows a Tip chord smaller than the Root chord and a Sweep length greater than 0mm, OR the visual representation of the rocket clearly shows non-rectangular, swept and tapered fins.

task_completion.description: Verify the rocket maintains a stability margin above 1.0 caliber.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket main window displays a stability margin (typically in the upper right corner or flight configuration panel) that is greater than 1.0 cal.

task_completion.description: Verify a flight simulation has been run with the new design.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket is open and shows at least one completed simulation run with populated data (e.g., Apogee, Max velocity, Max acceleration).

task_completion.description: Verify the modified rocket design is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog pointing to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/simple_model_rocket.ork', a file manager/terminal showing the file was recently updated, or the OpenRocket window title showing the correct filename without an unsaved indicator (*).

task_completion.description: Verify a multi-paragraph analysis report was written documenting the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying a multi-paragraph report that explicitly mentions original dimensions (150mm), new fin dimensions, the stability margin assessment, and simulated performance changes.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenRocket application open and being interacted with, rather than just a text editor modifying the raw .ork XML file.

integrity.description: Ensure the simulation results in the report match actual OpenRocket simulation outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The performance numbers (like apogee or velocity) mentioned in the text report should plausibly match the numbers visible in the OpenRocket 'Flight simulations' tab screenshots.

integrity.description: Ensure the report contains actual analysis and is not just a copy-paste of the task prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the report should contain specific numbers and analytical sentences generated by the agent, not just a repetition of the prompt's instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target rocket file is '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/simple_model_rocket.ork'. The original fins were 150x150mm rectangular fins with 0mm sweep. The report must be saved to one of the following paths: '/home/ga/Documents/exports/fin_redesign_report.txt', '/home/ga/Documents/exports/report.txt', '/home/ga/Documents/fin_redesign_report.txt', or '/home/ga/fin_redesign_report.txt'. The passing threshold is 60/100 points.
